text:
Once upon a dimly lit medieval market, a small figure named Olly slipped into a leather booth draped with an ox hide.

Inside, a peculiar old foxerman bartered bird feathers for fancy feathers. Olly had heard tales of these feathered wonders.

"Care for a feathered friend?" the foxerman asked, plucking an unusually vivid plumage.

"I'd adore something as intriguing as that," Olly said gingerly.

The foxerman smiled. "Would you fancy a bit of fine bird song?"

"Absolutely," Olly responded with excitement.

The foxement took out a small, ornately carved flute, filled with seeds that twinkled with tiny lights. With a flourish, the foxeman blew a melody so sweet it seemed birds had assembled just for that moment.

As the song continued, feathers danced across the booth, weaving intricate patterns. Olly was enraptured, lulled into a serene trance.

That quiet spell ended swiftly when a hawk swooped into the niche above
